# Pseudopotentials for cube4siesta integration tests

SIESTA-native `.psf` (Froyen/ATOM) pseudopotentials, GGA-PBE.

## Source

Downloaded from the **Cornell NNIN Virtual Vault**:
https://nninc.cnf.cornell.edu/psplist.php?element=<symbol>

The Vault is a long-standing community redistribution of
pseudopotentials tested with a range of DFT codes, including SIESTA.
These files are generated by the ATOM code (Troullier-Martins scheme)
and are freely redistributable under the Vault's public terms.

## Files

| Element | Valence config (from psf header) | N_valence |
|---------|----------------------------------|-----------|
| C    | 2s^2 2p^2                    | 4  |
| Si   | 3s^2 3p^2                    | 4  |
| V    | 4s^2 3d^3                    | 5  |
| S    | 3s^2 3p^4                    | 6  |
| Se   | 4s^2 4p^4                    | 6  |
| Mn   | 4s^2 3d^5                    | 7  |
| Te   | 5s^2 5p^4                    | 6  |

## Note on cross-code restart

When using these to restart from another code's density, **check that
the source code uses the same core/valence partition**. In particular:
- OpenMX default V_PBE19 treats V 3s/3p as valence (13 val. e-) while
  this `V.psf` freezes them in the core (5 val. e-). A cube from the
  former is not directly usable as a .RHOIN for a SIESTA run using the
  latter; see `examples/vsse_openmx/` for a worked example.

## Historical note

An alternative PSML (pseudo-dojo) set was originally downloaded here
too, but SIESTA 4.1.5 does not support PSML natively (PSML support
arrived in SIESTA 5.x), so that set was removed in favor of direct
`.psf` files from Cornell NNIN.
